summary: The implementation successfully delivers all seven requirements with complete backend storage, AI-powered analysis, manual reporting, interactive mapping, history views, and emergency-themed UI. All acceptance criteria are met with evidence of implementation across frontend and backend files.

hallucinated_features.description: Live camera monitoring feature with real-time obstacle detection and classification
hallucinated_features.reason: The BuildRequest explicitly excludes real-time features, live GPS tracking, and driver alerts. LiveCameraSection.tsx implements live camera monitoring with obstacle tracking and motion classification, which was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Road surface detection and analysis (potholes, wet surfaces, damage indicators)
hallucinated_features.reason: The BuildRequest focuses solely on accident detection through manual reporting and image analysis. Extensive road surface feature extraction including pothole detection, wet surface warnings, and damage indicators was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Driver alert system with obstacle warnings and speed limit detection
hallucinated_features.reason: The BuildRequest explicitly states in constraints and non-goals: 'Do not create driver alert system or 100-meter proximity warnings (explicitly removed from scope)' and 'Real-time driver alerts based on proximity to accidents' are non-goals.

hallucinated_features.description: Complex backend types for DetectionResult, ObstacleEvent, EmergencyEvent, and SpeedLimitDetection
hallucinated_features.reason: The backend should store AccidentRecord types only per REQ-5. The implementation includes extensive unrelated detection history types (DetectionResult with road surface analysis, ObstacleEvent, EmergencyEvent, SpeedLimitDetection) that are not part of the accident detection system scope.
No requirement specifies PDF report generation. This appears to be carried over from the original road detection system.
